perturbation.original_text: The purpose of the disclosure of Confidential Information is to enable the Target’s assets and liabilities relev ant to the Proposed Transaction to be assessed.
perturbation.changed_text: The purpose of the disclosure of Confidential Information is to enable the Target’s assets and liabilities relev ant to the Proposed Transaction to be assessed and the performance capabilities to be certified.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'and the performance capabilities to be certified' broadens the purpose of disclosure beyond merely assessing assets and liabilities. This new purpose may require the Receiving Party to take actions not explicitly covered in the confidentiality obligations, creating a potential conflict. The contradiction lies in the implied extended use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party may use the Confidential Information solely for th e purposes of assessing the Target for purposes of the Proposed Transaction. T he Receiving Party recognises that this Agreement imposes an affirmative duty to hold such information in confidence and protect it from dissemination to and use by any unauthorised persons. In the absence of the Disclosing Party’s prior wr itten consent, the Receiving Party shall not produce nor disclose the Confidential Information, or any part thereof, to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the purpose of confidential information disclosure to include 'performance capabilities to be certified,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text that explicitly limits the use of confidential information 'solely for the purposes of assessing the Target for purposes of the Proposed Transaction.' The original text only allowed for assessment, and the changed text expands it to certification, thus creating a strong contradiction because the receiving party is now potentially obliged to do more than assess.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party retains all right, title and interest in the Confidential Information and any reasonably related information which may be subsequently developed, and the Receiving Party waives all rights to the intellectual pr operty and copyright therein in favour of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party retains all right, title and interest in the Confidential Information and any reasonably related information which may be subsequently developed, unless a separate IP agreement is enacted, and the Receiving Party waives all rights to the intellectual pr operty and copyright therein in favour of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless a separate IP agreement is enacted' creates a contingency where the Disclosing Party might *not* retain all rights. This contradicts the initial, absolute statement of ownership. Enforcement is uncertain because the existence/terms of the separate IP agreement are undefined. This phrase directly contradicts the first phrase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information is and shall be considered valuable trade secrets owned exclusively by the Di sclosing Party. The Disclosing Party retains all right, title and interest in the Confidential Information and any reasonably related information which may be subsequently developed, unless a separate IP agreement is enacted, and the Receiving Party waives all rights to the intellectual pr operty and copyright therein in favour of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a contingency ('unless a separate IP agreement is enacted') to the Disclosing Party's ownership of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original, absolute statement of ownership in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces uncertainty about ownership and enforceability, which aligns with the Perturbation Explanation. The initial phrase states the Disclosing Party retains all rights, whereas the added phrase creates a condition where they might not.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly following the request of the Disclosing Party, the Rece iving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party all confidential information (and copies and ex tracts thereof) furnished to, or created by or on behalf of, the Receiving Part y. Notwithstanding the return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly following the request of the Disclosing Party, the Rece iving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party all confidential information (and copies and ex tracts thereof) furnished to, or created by or on behalf of, the Receiving Part y, unless such information is required for ongoing legal proceedings. Notwithstanding the return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless such information is required for ongoing legal proceedings' creates an exception to the return obligation. This directly contradicts the initial requirement to deliver *all* confidential information. The contradiction is that the Receiving Party can now potentially withhold information. There is an in-text contradiction within the section itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. Promptly following the request of the Disclosing Party, the Rece iving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party all confidential information (and copies and ex tracts thereof) furnished to, or created by or on behalf of, the Receiving Part y, unless such information is required for ongoing legal proceedings. Notwithstanding the return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless such information is required for ongoing legal proceedings' which creates an exception to the requirement to deliver *all* confidential information. The original text requires complete delivery, while the changed text allows withholding under specific circumstances. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, and the explanation states there is an in-text contradiction within the section itself. The change creates a strong and direct conflict regarding the Receiving Party's obligation to return confidential information, hence YES.
